label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the passage and determine which question has not been discussed. The options are: (A) Why are men smarter than women? (B) Why are men more stupid than women? (C) How does the result go along with the past research? (D) How can we help the men in the bottom? First, I'll scan through the passage for each of these questions. For (A) "Why are men smarter than women?" - The passage says that men have always believed they are smarter than women, but it doesn't really give a reason why. It does say there's a study showing that men are in the top 2% more than women, but the focus is more on the distribution and not the reason why men might be smarter. For (B) "Why are men more stupid than women?" - The passage mentions the study found more men in the bottom 2%, and it suggests a possible reason related to societal expectations and risk-taking behavior. It also quotes Professor Bates suggesting that the female developmental program may be more towards ensuring survival and the middle ground, implying that men may take more risks in their learning, leading to more at the extremes. For (C) "How does the result go along with the past research?" - The passage mentions that the research aligns with past results showing that men are more likely to receive first or third class degrees while women secure the seconds. It also discusses a past study that showed women securing more firsts and seconds, while men are getting more thirds. For (D) "How can we help the men in the bottom?" - The passage doesn't discuss any interventions or solutions for helping men who are in the bottom. It just presents the findings and possible reasons but doesn't suggest any specific help
